figures.caption: Table 1. Comparison results with PETL (Middle) and METL (Bottom) approaches across distinct network architectures. Best results are in bold, and second best results are underlined.

Table 2. Comparison results with PETL (Top) and METL (Middle, Bottom) methods on GLUE. Ours♡ and Ours♠ indicate our method implemented based on UniPT and SHERL, respectively. Best results are in bold, and second best results are underlined.

Table 4. Comparison results with METL approaches across various architectures and distinct VL tasks. Ours♡ and Ours♠ denote our implementations based on UniPT and SHERL, respectively. Best results are in bold, and second best results are underlined.
Table 3. Comparison results with PETL (Top) and METL (Bottom) methods on VTAB-1K. Best results are in bold, and second best results are underlined.

Table 5. Ablation study on various distillation strategies that are applied to distinct structures (i.e. ‘logits-based’ and ‘feature-based’) with different distillation directions, where ‘S’ and ‘B’ refer to side network and backbone, respectively. Best results are in bold, and the second best ones are underlined.
Table 6. Ablation study on fading the side network during inference on distinct datasets for the ITR task. Best results are in bold, and the second best ones are underlined.

Figure 1. Illustration on our method. Part (I) shows the pipeline of Masked Dual Path Distillation. During training, the backbone and side networks mutually act as teacher and student for effective distillation, where side networks are abandoned after training for inference efficiency. Part (II) displays Hierarchical Feature-based Distillation that adopt distinct strategies across different layers. It particularly selects a set of masked features, making them learnable with guidance of backbones in deep layers.
